summary: Matched a focused batch of Crazy Hand fighter functions in ftCh_Init.c, split out from PR #2024. The PR replaced several m2c placeholder comments with C implementations for Crazy Hand initialization, damage physics, and animation-end handlers, added missing declarations/externs in ftCh_Init.h, and corrected ftCrazyHandAttributes.x10 from s32 to float after review feedback. The work improved Crazy Hand decomp coverage while also documenting a reusable lesson: prefer typed motion-vars/attribute fields over raw pointer casts when matching fighter code.
what_changed: Implemented ftCh_Init_80155FCC as a stage-dependent dispatcher: if Stage_80225194() returns 0xFB it calls ftCh_Init_80156310(gobj), otherwise it calls ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B174(gobj).
Implemented fn_80157080 to change Crazy Hand motion state to 0x158 using ftCh_Init_804DA070 and ftCh_Init_804DA074, then call ftAnim_8006EBA4.

Implemented ftCh_Damage2_Phys using GET_FIGHTER, ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs from fp->ft_data->ext_attr, ft_80085134, and ftBossLib_8015BE40 with fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC, fp->mv.ch.unk0.x18, attrs->x14, and attrs->x10.
Implemented ftCh_BackCrush_Anim so that when animation frames are finished it calls the function pointer stored at fp->mv.ch.unk0.x4(gobj).
Implemented ftCh_BackDisappear_Anim so that when animation frames are finished it zeroes fp->self_vel.x and calls ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015BC88(gobj).
Implemented ftCh_Cancel_Anim so that when animation frames are finished it zeroes fp->self_vel.x and calls ftCh_Init_8015A184(gobj).
Added needed includes for ft/fighter.h and gr/stage.h in ftCh_Init.c.
Added prototypes/extern declarations for ftCh_Init_8015A184, ftCh_Init_804DA070, and ftCh_Init_804DA074 in ftCh_Init.h.
Changed ftCrazyHandAttributes.x10 from s32 to float in types.h, aligning the field type with its actual use in ftBossLib_8015BE40.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scoped to a small Crazy Hand batch split from a larger PR, making review focused on six claimed function matches.
Used existing fighter abstractions such as GET_FIGHTER, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, and typed Crazy Hand motion variables instead of leaving raw offsets in the final diff.
Responded to review by correcting the attribute struct type instead of preserving a cast at the callsite, improving future readability and type safety.
Modeled animation-end handlers consistently: check ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, then perform the terminal transition or callback.
Moved magic global float references into header externs with addresses, making the decompiled function and declarations consistent.
decomp_lessons: If an attribute field is repeatedly cast to a float, update the character attribute struct field type when evidence supports it; do not hide the mismatch with pointer casts.
For fighter per-action temporary state, prefer typed motion variable fields such as fp->mv.ch.unk0 members over raw byte-offset access into Fighter.
Animation callbacks for boss/fighter states often match cleanly as an inverted ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining check followed by a state transition, velocity reset, or function-pointer callback.
Stage-specific behavior can be expressed directly once the relevant stage query is identified; here Stage_80225194() == 0xFB selected a different Crazy Hand initialization path.
When matching C uses existing globals such as ftCh_Init_804DA070 and ftCh_Init_804DA074, add proper extern declarations rather than relying only on local declarations.
naming_conventions: Crazy Hand symbols use the ftCh_ prefix, for example ftCh_Damage2_Phys, ftCh_BackCrush_Anim, and ftCh_Cancel_Anim.
Unidentified address-based functions remain named with the ftCh_Init_80xxxxxx or fn_80xxxxxx pattern when no semantic name is established.
Character-specific attribute typedef uses ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs / ftCrazyHandAttributes style.
Header declarations preserve address comments such as /* 15A184 */ and /* 4DA070 */ for symbol traceability.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched ftCh_Damage2_Phys by using typed motion-vars fields fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC and fp->mv.ch.unk0.x18 as arguments to ftBossLib_8015BE40, avoiding raw offsets like fp + 0x234C and fp + 0x2358.
Matched float attribute usage by changing attrs->x10 to float, eliminating the need for *(float*)&attrs->x10.
Used direct motion-state constants and known float globals in Fighter_ChangeMotionState to match fn_80157080.
Used function-pointer invocation through fp->mv.ch.unk0.x4 for ftCh_BackCrush_Anim, indicating that Crazy Hand motion vars hold callbacks/state data.
Used zeroing of fp->self_vel.x before transition calls in BackDisappear and Cancel animation handlers.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Update ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs so attrs->x10 is a float instead of casting the pointer at the ftBossLib_8015BE40 callsite.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Accesses into Fighter should use M2C_FIELD, or preferably typed members from union ftCrazyHand_MotionVars following examples in ft/types.h for other characters.

follow_up_queries: Find other Crazy Hand functions still using raw Fighter offsets around 0x234C or 0x2358 and convert them to ftCrazyHand motion-vars fields if supported.
Audit ftCrazyHandAttributes fields near x10/x14/x18/x1C for additional type corrections based on callsite usage.
Search for remaining Crazy Hand animation handlers that are simple ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining terminal transitions.
Compare ftCrazyHand_MotionVars layout against other boss/character motion var unions in ft/types.h for naming opportunities.
Check whether ftCh_Init_804DA070 and ftCh_Init_804DA074 can receive semantic names once their data use is better understood.
